title: Ti (III) Reagent
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f4ov0","text":"Used for washing off extracellular iron.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a24rk","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6r2e","text":"Please contact Dr. Steven Wilhelm (wilhelm@utk.edu) for any additional information regarding this protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"ekaro","text":"To prepare reagent, combine chelator solution and salts in a glass-stoppered bottle.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"1v5hj","text":"Avoid stirring the solution for too long. Ti reacts with air very rapidly and will lose its reactivity.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9kb5v","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4iogj","text":"When finished, fill airspace with N2 before parafilming for storage.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":35,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3imab","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bi2m3","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"djhld","text":"See SDS (Safety Data Sheet) for safety warnings and hazards.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Per 250 mL, add 4.65 g of Na 2 EDTA into distilled or Milli-Q water. undetermined, Na2EDTA
Add 3.68 g of Na 3 Citrate. undetermined, Na3 Citrate
Add 2.5 mL of 0.1 M KCl. undetermined, of 0.1 M KCl
Add 3.0 g of NaCl (s). undetermined, NaCl
Add 7.77 mL of 20% TiCl 3 while stirring after salts and chelator solution are combined in glass-stoppered bottle. undetermined, 20% TiCl3
Using 10 M NaOH, adjust pH to 8. Add NaOH dropwise while stirring to attain desired pH, let equilibrate 30-60 minutes and re-check pH. 00:30:00, equilibration

To prepare reagent, combine chelator solution and salts in a glass-stoppered bottle. This will be the step where the 20% TiCl 3 is added in while stirring. The final result should be a clear purple solution.
creator: Ashley A Humphrey
